description:
Post-finalization spec revision 2026-06-06 (bl-2e26 §0/§1/§4/§12). The trusted default capability set is config-time, not run-time.

WHY: 'all config is RCE, explicitly installed' + 'core reads only landing config' means there must be NO runtime plugin magic. Instead, defaults live in a real folder that prime copies in — so the tracker/delivery/builtin plugins are ordinary landing entries, and the default set is swappable policy (an org ships its own seed) rather than core code (severability).

WHAT:
- Ship an app-level default-config/ folder at $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/balls/default-config/ (§1). Its install-default version wires tracker + delivery + builtin plugins and a default balls.toml (tasks_branch=balls/tasks, id_scheme, etc.).
- prime's local-miss/substrate path SEEDS a fresh landing by copying default-config/ into balls/config (same path-copy as install). On an established landing the seed step is a no-op.
- Remove any run-time default plugin wiring / 'run the default plugin (tracker)' code path — core only ever runs what is in the landing list (§0).
- Make the seed folder replaceable (org override) without touching core.

Pairs with bl-f970 (substrate creates the branches + invokes seed) and bl-30e2 (config defaults now come from the seed, serde is last-ditch fallback).
title: Greenfield: ship app default-config/ seed folder; prime seeds a fresh landing from it; retire run-time plugin defaults
